assertions.description: PerformanceObserver exists so visitors can observe script compile/eval timing.
PerformanceObserver.supportedEntryTypes reports the 'resource' entry type used to measure scripts.
PerformanceObserver.supportedEntryTypes reports the 'navigation' entry type, which carries page-level scripting timing in supporting browsers.
performance.getEntriesByType returns an array, used by callers to inspect script-load timing.
Inline classic <script> elements execute synchronously, the precondition for inline-script bytecode caching to be observable.
PerformanceNavigationTiming entries expose a .type property, the field used by code that tracks cold-vs-warm loads.
